75131.subject: Russia Hacked A Dissident’s iPhone With Banned Israeli Tech, Records Show
75131.body:
Forbes P.M.

Автор: Forbes


Автор: Forbes

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/russia-hacked-dissidents-iphone-banned-israeli-tech-records-vnigc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BJ3PNbzruR6au3rpgIZ15og%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0Q4d2ht-P0kck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqvfpphe~ng-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqvfpphe-ng
  
Good evening, In 2021, Israeli police tech provider Cellebrite announced that it would no longer sell its hacking technology to Russia. However, within months of the ban being enacted, Moscow investigators reportedly used the tech to search the iPhone of Russian opposition politician Andrey Pivovarov. Cellebrite has been widely criticized for previously selling its technology to regimes with poor human rights records. Here’s what we know. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads Nathan Posner/Anadolu via Getty Images OpenAI Rolls Out Powerful GPT-5.6 Models To Limited Users Vetted By U.S. Government The advanced technology is only available to select “trusted partners” for now. Getty Images Illinois Man Sues DraftKings For Allegedly Fueling Gambling Addiction The man alleges the betting firm caused him to lose his job and drain more than $2 million in assets, including his wedding fund.

75130.subject: 🟪 Friday Charts
75130.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9f199753-ca31-4e3d-9435-5e12896ecff5/BD_NL_Banner_Heading.png?t=1781793903)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e60d7cde-4250-41f8-9443-28ed51b80560/divider_transparent.png?t=1774649182)
Caption: 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3bd8a492-ff61-41db-a7f2-c821d481fd72/BD_NL_QuoteCircle.png?t=1781794162)
Caption: 

“==Let's keep a sense of crisis.”==
_— SK Hynix CEO Kwak Noh-jung_



----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/69fdc6e0-c728-445d-a96b-df412fa60cd4/image.png?t=1782506105)
Caption: 

# Friday charts: Gods of memory

SK Hynix, the South Korean maker of DRAM memory chips, allocates a generous 10% of its operating profits to its bonus pool. 

With business booming, that’s [estimated](https://www.koreajoongangdaily.com/opinion/debate-grows-over-semiconductor-bonuses/12560475) to work out to an average bonus of about _$477,000_ for each of its 34,500 employees. Barring sudden setbacks, next year’s bonus could be close to $1 million.

SK Hynix pays bonuses in cash, not shares.

This will reshape South Korea’s finances. In each of the next few years, SK Hynix is [likely](https://en.fnnews.com/news/202604091540595859) to pay at least $30 billion in corporate taxes. South Korea’s national debt is only $900 billion.

It’s reshaping the social scene in Korea, too.

Matchmakers [say](https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/south-korea-job-or-partner-samsung-sk-hynix-is-new-a-catch-2026-06-23/) employees at SK Hynix have risen to the top of the dating pool. A vest with the company logo has become “the ultimate blind date outfit” and Korea’s “hottest status symbol.” An SNL skit [labeled](https://biz.chosun.com/en/en-society/2026/04/29/LFK5GO34ABAFNNQG2AV5DPFBRA/) the company’s employees “Hynix gods.”

This is an unexpected turn of events.

DRAM is a commodity product typically mired in a state of severe oversupply: it’s the most cyclical part of the famously cyclical semiconductor industry. This has made it a terrible business to be in for most of history.

In 2001, Hynix had to be rescued from the brink of bankruptcy by its creditor banks. Several attempts to sell the company subsequently failed before SK Group finally bought it at a distressed price in 2012. As recently as 2023, the company posted a loss of 7.7 trillion won.

Things couldn’t look much more different today. The company’s expected to make a profit of as much as 80 trillion won this year. Next year should be even better.

Probably the year after that, too, because DRAM is suddenly one of the scarcest resources in the AI e

This week’s highlights: Securitize NYSE listing timeline; Continental partners with Securitize; Securitize to Tokenize Atlas America Fund into USAFi; Tokenization tops $51B; and KDDI and Securitize Japan explore next-generation financial services. Securitize and Cantor Equity Partners II Combination Expected to Raise ~$400M and Trade on NYSE July 2 | Securitize Securitize’s proposed business combination with Cantor Equity Partners II moves closer to completion, with expected proceeds and NYSE timing under ticker SECZ now outlined. READ MORE Continental Partners With Securitize for Digital Securities | Crypto News With a 20-year track record as the gold standard in SPAC processing, Continental’s partnership with Securitize helps bring tokenization capabilities to a broader segment of the market. READ MORE Crypto Critic Nouriel Roubini Partners With Securitize to Launch Tokenized Atlas America Fund | Crypto Briefing The planned launch brings together Atlas Capital Team and Securitize’s tokenization platform to link an SEC-registered ETF to Dubai’s VARA framework as tokenized RWA collateral. READ MORE Tokenized RWA Market Cap Rises 40% to Top $51B as Industry Races to Define Equity Tokenization Model: Bernstein | The Block Tokenized RWAs have crossed $51 billion in market cap, up 40% year-to-date, even as the broader crypto market has lost roughly a fifth of its value over the same period, according to Bernstein. READ MORE KDDI and Securitize Japan Explore Next-Generation Financial Services Leveraging Blockchain  | Securitize As KDDI expands its next-generation financial services strategy, it will work with Securitize Japan to explore RWA tokenization use cases across telecom and financial services. READ MORE

75128.subject: 🔷 The Daily: Senators call for Polymarket probe
75128.body:
Framework Ventures raises $400 million for fourth fund to invest across crypto, AI and robotics | BitGo cuts 15% of staff to refocus on AI infrastructure and stablecoins | Sharplink buys Ethereum for first time in 8 months, adding 5,000 ETH: onchain analyst | Aave founder Stani Kulechov says AAVE isn't for sale 'at a 70% discount' following report of Payward bid |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Kyle Baird

Presented by

Figure Markets ( https://figuremarkets.onelink.me/Plnq/theblocknewsletter?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Friday 🎉 *Bitcoin* briefly slid to its lowest level since late 2024 as sticky inflation and persistent spot ETF outflows weighed on sentiment, with analysts ( https://www.theblock.co/post/406340/bitcoins-fragile-floor-cracks-as-fed-hawks-circle-and-etf-investors-keep-pulling-out-analysts?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) divided on whether the market has found a cycle bottom.

In today's newsletter, two U.S. senators call for a *Polymarket* probe, *Framework Ventures* raises $400 million for its fourth fund, *BitGo* cuts 15% of its staff, and more.

Meanwhile, *Spain* says ( https://www.theblock.co/post/406362/spain-says-no-exceptions-extensions-binance-other-crypto-firms-mica-deadline?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) there are "no exceptions or extensions" for *Binance* or other crypto firms ahead of the *MiCA* deadline. Plus, *Base* suffers ( https://www.theblock.co/post/406409/base-suffers-second-mainnet-stall-in-two-days?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) a second mainnet stall in two days.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out *The Starting Block* ( https://www.youtube.com/@theblockcrypto?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) : Catch an exclusive, in-person livestream interview with *Binance* founder *Changpeng "CZ" Zhao* as *The Block* launches its new daily live show on *X* and *YouTube* on June 29 at 8 a.m. ET.

*THE BIG ONE*

*🕵️ Bipartisan senators call for CFTC investigation into Polymarket after fake bets report*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/406400/bipartisan-senators-call-cftc-investigation-polymarket-fake-bets?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Sens. *John Curtis* , R-Utah, and *Adam Schiff* , D-Calif., asked *CFTC* Chair *Michael Selig* on Thursday to investigate *Polymarket* over alleged staged bets.

* The senators said the agency is neither enforcing the law properly nor equipped to act as a federal gambling regulator.
* Their letter follows a Wall Street Journal report that said Polymarket paid creators to film fake bets, sometimes faking wins on replica sites.
* The WSJ reviewed 1,105 videos from 10 creators and said none of t

75126.subject: To Infinity and Beyond
75126.body:
In Context

Автор: J.P. Morgan


Автор: J.P. Morgan

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/infinity-beyond-jpmorgan-xrb7e?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B8YSZBnQJRteJI7gL8M9PmA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1QuUYa_f8ckck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqv8yxg9~6t-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqv8yxg9-6t
  
🚀 In light of SpaceX’s historic IPO, discover how the space economy is becoming a launchpad for growth. ⚽ Which sectors stand to outperform during the 2026 FIFA World Cup? 🔐 Discover why investing in strategic technology is critical for security and resiliency. Want more content from In Context? Sign up here to receive weekly insights directly in your inbox. What’s propelling the space economy? The space economy is transitioning from a niche sector to a launchpad for growth. Historically, much of space startup investment was directed at space access. Spacecraft manufacturing and launches alone accounted for over half of all deals globally, and the infrastructure phase was dominated by private markets and venture capital. The use-case phase, by contrast, has the potential to touch public companies across a range of established industries, making the space theme more investable for public market participants. Defense demand, commercial investment and the possibility of space-based solar power (SBSP) are bringing the third age of the space economy into focus. What’s next for the industry? Prepare for liftoff 🔗 By the numbers $250B As per the World Economic Forum, the space defense market could be worth $250 billion by 2035 as countries seek to build out orbital defense systems. $910M J.P. Morgan Global Research estimates that the World Cup could generate $910 million in incremental U.S. hotel room revenue. $18T According to data from S&P Global, private markets assets under management (AUM) is projected to exceed $18 trillion by 2027. 2026 FIFA World Cup: Could equity markets score big? The tournament is poised to generate a surge in consumption, which could result in a windfall for consumer-facing sectors such as accommodation and advertising. Get in the game 🔗 Powering the AI revolution AI is increasingly tied to physical infrastructure, from data centers to supply chains. It’s creating new pressure points, but also new opportunities — and how it scales and who it serves is still evolving. Watch more 📹 What’s driving financial sponsors to diversify? For decades, financial sponsors followed a familiar playbook: acquire companies, improve operations and exit through public markets or strategic sales. However, the landscape is shifting. Explore the factors 🔗 Want more content from In Context? 📩 Sign up here to receive weekly insights directly in your inbox.
